[2026-04-09T08:22:35.462Z] agent: Hi Saru, I’m Sara, your interview coach from Intervoo.  
To start, what job role or interview are you mainly preparing for right now?
[2026-04-09T08:23:01.315Z] agent: No worries if you're thinking or feeling unsure, Saru.  
For now, can you tell me what kind of work or job interests you even a little bit?
[2026-04-09T08:23:22.495Z] user: Uh, I like to find bugs, so I chose a part as in QA engineering and I'm studying manual testing and I tested my PSU exit also.
[2026-04-09T08:23:25.129Z] agent: Nice, that’s really clear — you enjoy finding bugs and you’re already doing manual testing, even on your PSU exit.  
What made you choose QA engineering specifically instead of something like development?
[2026-04-09T08:24:37.220Z] agent: It’s okay, take your time — I understand you.  
When you imagine yourself working as a QA engineer in a company, what part of that work feels most exciting to you?
[2026-04-09T08:24:59.893Z] user: Finding a bug and fighting with the developer is the most exciting thing, and we are the advocate of the customer. So,
[2026-04-09T08:25:03.389Z] user: I want to see in a customer perspective.
[2026-04-09T08:25:09.159Z] user: So I think a lot, so also I learn more and it's a very exciting portal.
[2026-04-09T08:25:11.589Z] agent: I love how you see yourself as the customer’s advocate and enjoy that challenge with developers.  
Looking ahead, what is your dream role or dream company as a QA engineer?
[2026-04-09T08:25:34.288Z] user: Now only I am starting my career in IT, so I don't have any.

[2026-04-09T08:25:50.293Z] user: And.

[2026-04-09T08:25:50.688Z] user: 12.

[2026-04-09T08:25:58.117Z] user: Future, I think so.

Now thinking about QA itself, what do you think a QA engineer actually does on a normal working day in a company?

What specific skills or tools do you think are most important for a QA engineer to learn?
[2026-04-09T08:27:42.490Z] user: Important skill is to logical thinking.
[2026-04-09T08:27:42.689Z] user: And.
[2026-04-09T08:27:55.133Z] user: And what tool you want to know means, uh, automation script, anything can be like Selenium Play write in one language. And you want to know that.
[2026-04-09T08:28:05.264Z] user: You want to know that how you are going to handle it and how your mindset is going to be on that.
[2026-04-09T08:28:07.748Z] agent: You’ve thought about this well — logical thinking and tools like Selenium or Playwright are very relevant for QA.  
Have you read any job descriptions for QA roles yet, and if yes, what things did you see again and again?
[2026-04-09T08:28:38.473Z] user: I see that all need automation testing and tools like Selenium, Play Red language like Java, Python, like that. I saw most and they need logical thinking also.
[2026-04-09T08:28:40.956Z] agent: Nice, you’ve clearly noticed how strongly companies ask for automation skills and logical thinking.

Thinking about a QA in a software company, what do you think they do on a normal working day?
[2026-04-09T08:32:16.499Z] user: As starting of the day they will

[2026-04-09T08:32:32.809Z] user: Date Link

[2026-04-09T08:34:29.804Z] agent: You can take a moment to think, no hurry.  
What is one skill or tool you feel a QA should definitely know?
[2026-04-09T08:34:43.535Z] user: The one skill is problem solving and logical thinking.
[2026-04-09T08:34:55.533Z] user: Because they have to think logically what's behind the application and what is behind this.
[2026-04-09T08:35:01.781Z] user: All and the tool is they have to know.
[2026-04-09T08:35:04.259Z] user: Automation Tool
